channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Missile Strike Kills 24 in Kyiv Apartment Building
item.description: A Russian missile struck a residential building in Kyiv, killing 24 people including three children. President Zelenskiy called for punishment of Moscow as rescue workers recovered bodies from the rubble.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Arrests Ukrainian National Suspected of Spying for Russia
item.description: German prosecutors announced the arrest of a Ukrainian national on suspicion of espionage for Russia. The case highlights ongoing intelligence threats in Europe amid the Ukraine war.

item.title: ⚔️ US Admiral: Iran Military Capability Dramatically Degraded
item.description: The US admiral commanding the Iran war effort said American strikes have left Tehran with only small or moderate ability to strike its neighbors, describing major damage to Irans defense industry.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran FM: No Trust in US, Will Negotiate Only If Serious
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said Tehran has no trust in Washington and will only negotiate if the US shows seriousness. He added that all vessels can pass through Hormuz except those at war with Iran.

item.title: 🇵🇱 US Cancels Planned Deployment of 4,000 Troops to Poland
item.description: The Pentagon scrapped plans to temporarily deploy 4,000 US-based troops to Poland, raising fresh questions about Trumps expected troop cuts in Europe and NATOs eastern flank security.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran says Pakistan mediation with US at impasse over uranium enrichment
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Pakistan-mediated talks with the US face deadlock over the enriched uranium issue, while welcoming potential Chinese diplomatic involvement.

item.title: 🌍 UN agencies warn Somalia faces renewed famine risk as hunger crisis deepens
item.description: Nearly 6 million people face acute food insecurity in Somalia with famine risk emerging in the southwest, as UN agencies call for urgent international assistance.

item.title: 🚁 Ukraine races to deploy AI-powered drone swarms to counter Russian manpower advantage
item.description: Ukrainian defense industry is advancing swarm drone technology that would allow few operators to deploy dozens or hundreds of autonomous attack craft, though mass battlefield deployment remains years away.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Economy Expected to Grow for Second Quarter Despite Iran War Shadow
item.description: Japan's GDP likely rose in January-March on recovering exports and solid domestic demand, a Reuters poll showed, though the full impact of the Iran conflict on the global economy has yet to be felt.

item.title: 🛢️ Gazprom Astrakhan Gas Plant Halted After Drone Attack
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike on May 13 forced Gazprom's Astrakhan gas processing plant to halt motor fuel production, though gas processing continues. The facility is one of Russia's largest.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraqi Parliament Approves Partial Government Led by PM Zaidi
item.description: Iraq's parliament approved a partial cabinet submitted by Prime Minister Ahmed al-Zaidi, ending months of political deadlock but leaving key security ministries unfilled amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇧🇴 Explosions Heard During Bolivia Mining Protest March
item.description: Explosions were reported during a march by mining groups in Bolivia demanding the resignation of President Luis Arce, amid ongoing political instability and economic crisis.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Trump Reaffirmed Ironclad US-Japan Alliance After China Trip
item.description: Japanese officials said Trump reaffirmed the US commitment to Japan's defense during his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping, as Tokyo seeks clarity on Trump's Asia strategy.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says He Wont Be Much More Patient With Iran
item.description: President Trump warned that his patience with Iran is running out as negotiations over Tehrans enriched uranium stockpile remain deadlocked, raising concerns about potential escalation.

item.title: 🛢️ Ukraine Doubles Strikes on Russian Oil Refineries This Year
item.description: Ukraine has significantly increased drone attacks on Russias energy infrastructure, doubling the number of oil refineries targeted since the start of the year, according to Russian officials.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India April Trade Deficit Hits $28.38 Billion
item.description: Indias merchandise trade deficit widened to $28.38 billion in April, reflecting the economic impact of Middle East tensions on oil imports and global supply chains.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump Discussed Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
item.description: President Trump said he discussed lifting sanctions on Chinese companies that purchase Iranian oil during his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping, with a decision expected soon.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Strikes Iranian Air Defense Systems Near Tehran
item.description: Israeli forces continued targeting Iranian military infrastructure, striking air defense systems near Tehran as part of ongoing operations to degrade Irans ability to threaten Israel.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia Rules Out LNG Export Curbs as East Coast Gas Supply Fears Ease
item.description: Australia decided against restricting LNG exports after domestic gas supply concerns on the east coast diminished, maintaining its position as a key global gas exporter.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump-Xi Summit Addresses AI and Semiconductor Controls
item.description: The Trump-Xi summit in Beijing included discussions on artificial intelligence and semiconductor export controls, key points of tension in the US-China technology rivalry.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Expects Double-Digit Billions in Chinese Farm Purchases After Summit
item.description: The US expects China to commit to double-digit billions in American agricultural purchases following the Trump-Xi summit, USTR Jamieson Greer said.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump and Xi Agree Iran Cannot Have Nuclear Weapons
item.description: President Trump said he and Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed that Iran cannot have nuclear weapons during their Beijing summit, while Xi offered to help negotiate with Tehran on Hormuz.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Agreed to Buy 200 Boeing Jets, Trump Says
item.description: President Trump announced that China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing aircraft during his summit with Xi Jinping, a potential boost for the American aerospace giant.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump-Xi Set Second Day of Talks After Taiwan Warning
item.description: President Trump left China with no major breakthroughs on trade or Iran, despite two days of warm talks. Xi warned Trump about Taiwan before the summit.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia and Ukraine Swap 205 Prisoners of War Each
item.description: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 205 prisoners of war each in the first stage of a larger swap agreement linked to a recent ceasefire brokered by President Trump.
